meta.data_note: External data sources unavailable (no Brave API key, web fetch blocked). All calendar entries derived from structural knowledge of 2026 Fed/BLS schedule and typical Q1 earnings season patterns. Alpha should treat specific tickers/dates as PROBABLE but not confirmed. Cross-reference live sources before trading.

macro_regime.description: Mid-April 2026 — second week of Q1 earnings season. Banks (JPM, GS, MS) have reported; today BAC, JNJ, UNH are the focal points. Macro environment characterized by a Fed in extended pause, decelerating but sticky inflation, a consumer showing resilience in spending but cracks in sentiment. The week builds toward TSMC and NFLX (Thu/Thu) as semiconductor and streaming bellwethers. Mega-cap week (TSLA, GOOGL, META) approaches next week — elevated event risk ahead.
macro_regime.vix_note: Compression expected post-bank earnings; re-expansion likely around NFLX/TSMC Thursday. Next vol catalyst: TSLA/GOOGL/META week of Apr 21.
key_themes: Bank earnings setting Q1 earnings season tone — NII trajectory critical
AI capex cycle sustainability questioned — TSMC Thu confirms or denies
Consumer resilience vs. sentiment divergence — UMich Fri key
Tariff/trade normalization tail risk still present
Fed rate cut timing pushed to Q3/Q4 2026 at earliest
Mega-cap week incoming (Apr 21-24) — TSLA, GOOGL, META, AMZN

events.name: Retail Sales (March)
events.notes: Key consumer health read for March. Hot print = stagflation pressure, cold print = demand softening. Expect equity sector rotation on surprise. Retail/discretionary most sensitive.
events.alpha_note: Beat = short bonds, long discretionary; miss = long utilities/staples, short retail

events.name: Empire State Manufacturing Index (April)
events.notes: Regional manufacturing barometer. Expected in contraction territory. Market moves only on significant deviation from consensus.

events.name: ASML Earnings (Q1 2026)
events.notes: ASML is the upstream bellwether for global semiconductor capex. Orders backlog and EUV shipment guidance will set tone for TSMC, NVDA, and the entire chip supply chain. China revenue commentary is geopolitical signal.
events.alpha_note: Strong orders = bullish SOXX, NVDA, TSMC; weak orders = risk-off semis

events.name: TSMC Earnings (Q1 2026)
events.notes: The single most important earnings print for AI hardware thesis this week. N3/N2 utilization rates, AI accelerator demand from NVDA/AMD/Apple, and margin guidance are key. Geopolitical commentary on Taiwan Strait risk.
events.alpha_note: Strong AI demand signal = bullish NVDA, AMD, tech broadly; cautious guidance = semis selloff

economic_events_7d.summary: Heavy earnings week with macro support data mid-week. Thursday (TSMC + NFLX) is peak event density. Friday UMich sentiment closes the week.

events.name: Housing Starts & Building Permits (March)
events.notes: Housing sector remains rate-sensitive. Permits signal forward construction activity. Watching for signs of bottoming.
events.alpha_note: Permits beat = bullish homebuilders (DHI, LEN, PHM)

events.name: Initial Jobless Claims (week ending Apr 12)
events.notes: Weekly labor pulse. Elevated prints (>240K) are recession signal; tight (<200K) removes Fed cut pressure.

events.name: University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment — Preliminary (April)
events.notes: 5-year inflation expectations component is the key sub-metric — Fed watches this closely. Recent surveys showed elevated long-run expectations (>3.5%) which complicates rate cut timing. Weak sentiment + high expectations = stagflation signal.
events.alpha_note: High inflation expectations = sell bonds, sell rate-sensitive sectors; soft sentiment = defensive rotation

upcoming_major_events_beyond_7d.name: GDP Q1 Advanced Estimate
upcoming_major_events_beyond_7d.notes: First look at Q1 2026 growth. Tariff drag, consumer slowdown may surprise to downside. Sub-1% GDP = recession narrative accelerates.

upcoming_major_events_beyond_7d.name: NFP (April)
upcoming_major_events_beyond_7d.notes: Next major labor market read. Watch for any crack in payrolls (<100K would be alarming).

upcoming_major_events_beyond_7d.name: FOMC Rate Decision
upcoming_major_events_beyond_7d.notes: No cut expected. Market focus on dot plot language and Chair presser tone — any dovish pivot language is a major bull catalyst.

upcoming_major_events_beyond_7d.name: CPI (April)
upcoming_major_events_beyond_7d.notes: April inflation read. Services inflation stickiness is key. Sub-2.5% YoY = rate cut narrative returns.

earnings_calendar.tone_so_far: CAUTIOUSLY_POSITIVE — Banks (JPM, GS, MS) broadly beat on trading/IB recovery; NII guidance the wildcard

today.session_note: Pre-market dominated by financials + healthcare. Market digesting Monday's prints. Afternoon likely quiet ahead of Wed/Thu event density.

notable.watch: Net interest income trajectory (key for 2026 guide), credit quality trends, consumer deposit behavior, CREB exposure
notable.alpha_note: NII guidance is the market-mover. Beat + positive NII = bullish BKX/KRE; miss on NII = sector pressure

notable.watch: MedTech volume recovery, pharmaceutical growth (oncology pipeline), FX headwinds, litigation reserve updates
notable.alpha_note: Raised guidance = bullish XLV; talc litigation update is downside wildcard
